meta.description: What's your burnout type? Take this viral quiz with 8 questions to discover your professional and emotional burnout pattern!
meta.keywords: burnout test, burnout type, stress test, work burnout, emotional burnout, professional burnout
meta.og_title: Burnout Test - What's Your Burnout Type?
meta.twitter_title: Burnout Test - What's Your Burnout Type?
meta.page_title: Burnout Test - What's Your Burnout Type? | DopaBrain

question.0: How do you spend your weekends?
question.0a: Catching up on work I didn't finish during the week
question.0c: Scrolling mindlessly, too bored to do anything meaningful
question.0d: Resting, but feeling guilty I'm not being productive
question.1: How do you feel first thing in the morning?
question.1a: Already checking emails before my feet hit the floor
question.1b: Dread. Just pure dread for the day ahead.
question.1c: Uninspired. Another day of the same routine.
question.2: A colleague needs support. What's your response?
question.2a: Drop everything to emotionally support them
question.2b: Offer to help with tasks (easier than feelings)
question.2c: Avoid interaction, I have nothing left to give
question.2d: Listen politely but feel completely empty inside
question.3: You get a work notification after hours. What happens?
question.3b: Ignore it but feel anxious about it all night
question.3c: Feel annoyed that my personal time is interrupted
question.3d: Feel drained by the constant digital intrusion
question.4: You finish a big task. How do you feel?
question.4a: Nothing feels like enough, already thinking about what's next
question.4b: Push myself harder to prove my worth
question.4d: Can't find the spark I used to have for my work
question.5: How are your creative ideas lately?
question.5a: Completely dried up. The well is empty.
question.5b: I don't care anymore. Ideas feel pointless.
question.5c: Force myself to produce even when uninspired
question.5d: Give my ideas away because I'm too tired to execute
question.6: Where does your energy come from these days?
question.6b: Faking it. I pretend I'm fine when I'm not.
question.6d: Screen time paradoxically drains me more
question.7: How do you feel about your work's purpose?
question.7a: Lost all sense of meaning or why I'm doing this
question.7b: Constantly trying to prove my worth and value
question.7c: Only find purpose in helping others, not myself
question.7d: Going through the motions without real connection

result.percentile: Only {pct} of all participants experience {type} burnout

overachiever.name: Overachiever Burnout
overachiever.description: You've built your identity around achievement, but the bar keeps rising. Rest feels like failure. You push through exhaustion because stopping means facing the terrifying question: who are you without your productivity? You're running on fumes, but you can't slow down because your self-worth is tied to output.

empathy.name: Empathy Burnout
empathy.tagline: Emotional labor has drained your tank
empathy.description: You've been the rock for everyone else while your own foundation crumbles. Caring professions, caregiving roles, or just being the 'therapist friend' has left you emotionally bankrupt. You absorb others' pain, but no one refills your cup. Compassion fatigue isn't weakness—it's what happens when you give more than you have.

boredom.name: Boredom Burnout
boredom.tagline: Repetitive tasks, zero growth, soul-crushing monotony
boredom.description: You're not overworked—you're under-challenged. The same tasks, same meetings, same nothing day after day. You've checked out mentally because there's nothing left to engage with. It's not laziness; it's your brain shutting down from chronic under-stimulation. You're rotting from stagnation.

invisible.name: Invisible Burnout
invisible.tagline: High-functioning on the outside, collapsing inside
invisible.description: You look fine. You perform well. No one knows you're barely holding it together. You've mastered the art of the polished exterior while internally, you're screaming. This is the most dangerous burnout because it's hidden—even from yourself. You're drowning in plain sight.

creative.name: Creative Burnout
creative.tagline: The well of ideas has run dry
creative.description: You used to overflow with inspiration. Now, there's nothing. The spark is gone. Every attempt to create feels forced, hollow, performative. You're not blocked—you're depleted. Your creative identity is crumbling because you've given everything to projects, clients, and demands, leaving nothing for the work that used to light you up.

digital.name: Digital Burnout
digital.description: Screen fatigue. Notification overload. Information avalanche. You're perpetually plugged in, and it's frying your circuits. Doom scrolling, constant connectivity, and the pressure to always respond has left you mentally exhausted. You're burned out not from working too hard, but from never truly disconnecting.

share.text: My burnout type is '{type}'! What's yours?
